arms_mandatory_baselines: k0 staging-identity reference: the byte-identical RULES-1 certificate over the byte-identical kernel TBox inside the shadow tree — INSTRUMENT gate only (PROPOSED-ASM-1405): must reproduce the pinned decision payload sha fce753bad1a9709ff7ff03b07dbd3e34651720575d4f5a72179e38d2b070aee5; executed pre-freeze WITHOUT unblinding (its outcome is already pinned in the frozen rules-1 record) — measured PASS 2026-07-12, re-measured PASS 2026-07-12 under the reworked attesting runner, report sha 8f4db5a78b91997df2da5a58cfcb1cd6c7316328bde53520cd319686c72e6648

k1 knull-plain substitution (THE PRIMARY ARM): data/axioms-v0 kinship trio + data/axioms-kinship-v1 REPLACED by poc/rules-1-knull/inputs/tbox-knull — a TBox compiled from plain-dictionary definitions (5 byte-verbatim from the knull v4 plain-authored store sha 97609abe17f87e10a384950a5d69d4e579e40935109573eaf782095bcb43c0d2; 5 newly authored in the same register), NO NSM explication, NO explicator/endorsement chain, knull terms minted under kot-knull-def/1 (PROPOSED-ASM-1400/1401/1402/1403); NOT executed pre-freeze (runner refuses without --registered on a FROZEN record, PROPOSED-ASM-1409)

k2 scrambled discrimination control: k1 with every constraint-slot filler Sattolo-deranged (seed 20260712; relation and class pools separately; record subjects fixed; identical topology/kinds/counts/token cost) — the content-destruction floor; MUST LOAD (/gates/k2_tbox_loads, PROPOSED-ASM-1412) AND then fail the survival predicate (/gates/discrimination_valid = k2_tbox_loads AND NOT k2_certificate_success, PROPOSED-ASM-1404) or the run is INSTRUMENT-INVALID — a k2 parse/load failure can NEVER read as successful discrimination; NOT executed pre-freeze (parse-only smoke excepted, ASM-1414)

phase-2b host-lift leg (CONDITIONAL, PROPOSED-ASM-1407/1417): A1 + A3(k=4) under the k1 TBox on the pinned rules-1 GPU harness bytes (rules1_runner.py 020394f2…, modal wrapper, rules1-manifest — see pins.harness_manifest), ACTIVATED ONLY when the pinned parent rules-1 analysis carries /analysis/primary_pass true (machine-checked by analysis/rules_1_knull.py --parent-analysis); kernel cells (kernel x A1, kernel x A3) REUSED verbatim from the frozen rules-1 campaign run-records, never regenerated; unevaluable => all hostlift fields null, never a fail

dependent_vars.definition: the pinned rules-1 certificate precondition predicate over the substituted TBox: success_asm_1131 AND gates_asm_1163_all_pass AND NOT kill_a_fired (deterministic, exhaustive, no sampling)
dependent_vars.name: certificate_survival

dependent_vars.definition: C_dec on stated / entailed cells and Wilson-LB95 soundness rates carried verbatim from the certificate bytes (descriptive components of the predicate)
dependent_vars.name: certificate_components

dependent_vars.definition: phase-2b only: exact match vs the pinned third-party CLUTRR gold (identical scoring to rules-1)
dependent_vars.name: item_correct_ext

independent_vars.name: seed (phase-2b hostlift leg only; certificate leg deterministic)

assumptions.claim: Substitution scope (PROPOSED-ASM-1400): ABox vocabulary URNs (mother/father/man/woman) are shared data schema; ALL definitional content substitutes — every constraint plus every TBox-introduced term (parent/person/grandparent/grandfather/grandmother, minted fresh under kot-knull-def/1). Across arms the SOURCE ABox, EVALUATION CONSTRUCTOR, and HARNESS BYTES are identical; the E1 generated cells are treatment-consistently alpha-renamed to each TBox's parent URN (harness-native, PROPOSED-ASM-1410) — the arms' evaluation cells are NOT byte-identical and this record never claims they are (review fix 5)

Contamination direction (PROPOSED-ASM-1401): the definition-drafting session had kernel exposure; the pressure is toward kernel-equivalence, i.e. toward the SURVIVES/deflationary branch — it cannot manufacture a false KERNEL-SPECIFIC (PASS) result; a PASS is therefore conservative, a NULL carries the contamination rider

Expected direction (PROPOSED-ASM-1406): kinship logic is common knowledge; a-priori SURVIVES is the likely branch; the record is run to MEASURE the MD-7 cap's resolution, not to confirm a preferred branch

Harness bytes are the FROZEN rules-1 bytes, re-verified at stage time (certificate.py, twin_engine.py, mint_kinship.py, items/world/world-v0 shas) — ERR_PIN_MISMATCH fail-closed; only the two TBox directories differ per arm, and their kot-corpus-hash/1 dir-digests are re-verified against THIS record's registered pins at stage time with a per-payload attestation block that the analysis validates (unattested or drifted payloads REJECTED => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, PROPOSED-ASM-1413)

Runtime/generation estimates (PROPOSED-ASM-1418, load_bearing:false): certificate leg ~4s CPU per arm at $0; phase-2b <=6k generations (858 items x 3 seeds x {A1, A3 k=4} on the knull cells only, kernel cells reused). EXTRAPOLATION from the rules-1/nsk1 cost data; resolution path: measured wall-clock + generation counts in the run provenance logs; never load-bearing for any verdict

follow_on_arms: k3 generic-provenance ruleset (public ontology-pattern transcription): PRE-REGISTERED AS A NAMED FOLLOW-ON ONLY, expected near-redundant with k1 (both compile common-knowledge kinship); running it requires a record amendment — NOT part of this campaign
n_planned.generations_estimate: certificate leg: 0 model calls (runtime estimate carried as EXTRAPOLATION PROPOSED-ASM-1418, load_bearing:false); phase-2b conditional leg: <=6k generations on the knull cells only (kernel cells reused from the frozen rules-1 campaign)

endpoints.test: PRIMARY (deterministic, exhaustive): does arm k1 (knull-plain TBox) satisfy the pinned rules-1 survival predicate success_asm_1131 AND gates_asm_1163_all_pass AND NOT kill_a_fired, computed by the byte-identical certificate? TRUE => /analysis/k1_survives => verdict NULL = KERNEL-NECESSITY-UNSHOWN-AGAINST-THIS-CHANNEL: kernel necessity is unshown against THIS ONE plain-dictionary compilation channel (this documented protocol, this drafting session) — one comparator licenses NO universal 'any competent ruleset' claim (review fix: NULL language); the MD-7/PROPOSED-ASM-1138 cap STAYS IN FORCE at the kinship vertical, re-worded to name the measured channel; kernel-specific value routes to authoring/endorsement economics, coverage at harder verticals, NL-robustness walls — equal prominence, the knull discipline verbatim. FALSE with all instrument gates green => /analysis/k1_collapses => verdict PASS = KERNEL-ADVANTAGE-OVER-THIS-KNULL-COMPILATION (review fix: PASS language), failure mode named mechanically in /analysis/k1_failure_mode; scope: this vertical, this compilation protocol, one knull author-channel — no claim that no better generic compilation exists.

k0 INSTRUMENT gate (PROPOSED-ASM-1405): the shadow-staged byte-identical harness over the byte-identical kernel TBox must reproduce the pinned rules-1 decision payload sha fce753ba… exactly — proves the staging instrument inert; failure => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, nothing licensed.

k2 discrimination INSTRUMENT gate (PROPOSED-ASM-1404/1412, review fix 3): /gates/discrimination_valid = k2_tbox_loads AND NOT k2_certificate_success — the Sattolo-scrambled TBox must LOAD AND then fail the survival predicate. Content destruction surviving the certificate means the instrument cannot distinguish content => INSTRUMENT-INVALID; a k2 parse/load failure => /gates/k2_tbox_loads false => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, NEVER 'successful discrimination' (an unparseable comparator proves nothing about content sensitivity).

compilation-fidelity INSTRUMENT gate (PROPOSED-ASM-1402/1414/1415, review fix 4), THREE mandatory components ANDed into /gates/compilation_lint_valid: (i) lint-report.json pass==true (every k1 constraint carries licensed_by quoting the plain-definition span; every slot URN in the shared-or-minted vocabulary); (ii) pre-freeze PARSE-ONLY smoke pass==true (ablation_runner.py --smoke: twin_engine.load_tbox over every arm's staged TBox — no closure, no decisions, no unblinding; parse-smoke.json pinned); (iii) INDEPENDENT definition->constraint mapping review pass==true (def-constraint-mapping.json reviewed by a role that authored neither the definitions nor the compiler; mapping-review.json pinned). Guards the strawman hazard: lexical span traceability alone does not establish a competent comparator or exclude compiler-induced collapse.

load-failure rule (PROPOSED-ASM-1408): if the knull TBox fails to load or the certificate crashes, that is an authoring-channel failure => INSTRUMENT-INVALID with named failure mode; NEVER scored as collapse (a revised compilation is a NEW record).

k2 load INSTRUMENT gate (PROPOSED-ASM-1412, review fix 3): the scrambled TBox must load and the certificate must run over it; a k2 parse/load failure resolves INSTRUMENT-INVALID — it can never be scored as successful discrimination (a trivially-unparseable knull ruleset must not masquerade as kernel-specificity).

phase-2b h1 (CONDITIONAL, PROPOSED-ASM-1407/1417): knull host lift (A3_knull - A1_knull) paired item BCa one-sided 95% LB > 0, B=10000, PRNG seed 20260712, AND Holm-adjusted one-sided bootstrap p within {h1,h2} (smallest p at alpha/2=0.025, other at 0.05 only if the first rejects — review fix 9, no unadjusted twin tests); requires EXACTLY 858 paired items x 3 seeds x all four (host_tbox x host_arm) cells (duplicates/missing => /analysis/hostlift_rows_valid false, all stats null); ACTIVATED only when the pinned parent rules-1 analysis carries /analysis/primary_pass true; kernel cells reused verbatim from the frozen rules-1 run-records; unevaluable => null, never a fail.

phase-2b h2 (CONDITIONAL): TOST — 90% BCa CI of (kernel lift - knull lift) inside ±0.05 AND Holm-adjusted TOST bootstrap p within {h1,h2} (review fix 9) => the HOST-LIFT also survives substitution; same row-exactness, activation and reuse discipline as h1; host-level survival = h1 AND h2; reported alongside the certificate-leg verdict, never replacing it.

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: Binding on ANY outcome: this record measures ONE substitution channel (plain-dictionary knull register, THIS documented compilation protocol, one drafting session) at ONE vertical (kinship, the RULES-1 closed rule inventory, proof depth <= 4, gold-parse only). NULL (survives) licenses exactly: a plain-dictionary-sourced ruleset compiled under the documented protocol reproduces the RULES-1 non-inertness certificate at this vertical — kernel necessity is UNSHOWN against this one channel and the MD-7 cap stays in force here with that wording; it does NOT license 'the kernel adds no value anywhere' nor any claim about ANY/every competent ruleset (authoring economics, endorsement discipline, coverage at verticals where plain definitions fail to compile, and NL-robustness remain unmeasured by this record). PASS (collapses) licenses exactly: KERNEL-ADVANTAGE-OVER-THIS-KNULL-COMPILATION — THIS substitution failed the certificate where the endorsed kernel passed, at THIS vertical against THIS channel; it does NOT license 'no generic ruleset can succeed' (k3 and better compilers are named follow-ons). The contamination rider (PROPOSED-ASM-1401) rides every NULL sentence: the definition author had kernel exposure; convergence may partly reflect contamination, which biases TOWARD the NULL branch. k1/k2 decisions were computed by the pinned Python twin only; the inherited sparq-reason conformance text does not apply to substituted arms (PROPOSED-ASM-1416). Phase-2b inherits the full rules-1 envelope verbatim (two rungs sign-only, this rule inventory, this vertical, no NL claim). Nothing here amends rules-1: its frozen record, certificate pins and envelope stand byte-identical; this record RESOLVES the wording of its claim cap only.
hypotheses: H-K1 (the question, two-sided by construction): the RULES-1 non-inertness certificate either SURVIVES the knull-plain TBox substitution (=> kernel necessity UNSHOWN against this one plain-dictionary compilation channel — the deflationary branch, equal prominence; NO universal any-ruleset claim) or COLLAPSES (=> KERNEL-ADVANTAGE-OVER-THIS-KNULL-COMPILATION at this vertical, failure mode named). No direction is favoured by the record; PROPOSED-ASM-1406 discloses that SURVIVES is the a-priori likely branch.

H-K2 (discrimination premise, gated not hypothesised): the Sattolo-scrambled twin LOADS (/gates/k2_tbox_loads) and then fails the survival predicate — content destruction at identical topology must be visible to the certificate, else the instrument is invalid; a k2 parse/load failure is INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never discrimination (PROPOSED-ASM-1412).

H-K3 (phase-2b, conditional): if rules-1's host-lift primary lands PASS (machine-checked from the pinned parent analysis), the knull-substituted host lift reproduces it (h1 sign + h2 TOST equivalence, Holm within {h1,h2} on bootstrap p-values) or fails to (host-level kernel advantage over this compilation), under the identical pinned runner bytes with kernel cells reused from the frozen rules-1 run-records.
kill_criterion_verbatim: This record carries NO independent kill of the RULES-1 line; it RESOLVES the MD-7/PROPOSED-ASM-1138 claim cap. Verdict NULL (k1 survives) fixes the rules-1 claim wording AT THE KINSHIP VERTICAL to 'machinery non-inert; kernel necessity UNSHOWN against the measured plain-dictionary compilation channel' — every future rules-1 citation must carry that wording; one comparator licenses NO universal any-ruleset claim, and kernel-specific-value claims at this vertical are DEAD unless a NEW record shows a channel this ablation missed. Verdict PASS (k1 collapses, gates green) = KERNEL-ADVANTAGE-OVER-THIS-KNULL-COMPILATION: the cap lifts to 'kernel advantage shown at the kinship vertical against the measured plain-dictionary channel' — never 'no generic ruleset can succeed'. INSTRUMENT-INVALID (any gate: harness/attestation pins, staging identity, compilation lint+smoke+mapping-review, k1 load, k2 LOAD, k2 discrimination) licenses NOTHING and leaves the MD-7 cap exactly as frozen in rules-1 — in particular a k2 parse/load failure is INSTRUMENT-INVALID, never discrimination evidence (review fix 3). Degeneracy discipline: gate failures can never read as PASS, NULL, or INCONCLUSIVE evidence. The deflationary outcome gets EQUAL prominence with any positive (the knull discipline verbatim).

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py

runner_constraints.engine_identity_note: PROPOSED-ASM-1416 (review fix 6): k1/k2 ablation decisions execute ONLY the pinned Python differential twin (poc/rules-1/twin_engine.py 399fcd8d…). The inherited certificate_result.engine_identity text ('BOTH engines … sparq-reason … EXACTLY agreed', commit 0ab87b2a) describes the RULES-1 conformance run over the KERNEL TBox and is FALSE for the substituted arms — sparq-reason is NOT executed over the knull/scrambled TBoxes. The runner stamps this disclosure into every k1/k2 payload (engine_identity_ablation_disclosure); substituted-TBox differential conformance is a NAMED FOLLOW-ON, not part of this record; no reader may cite the inherited text for k1/k2.
runner_constraints.hardware: local CPU (2 shared cores) for the certificate leg — ~4s per arm, $0; single A10G via Modal ONLY for the conditional phase-2b leg
runner_constraints.harness: poc/rules-1-knull (ablation_runner.py shadow-staging + the BYTE-IDENTICAL poc/rules-1 certificate bytes; poc/rules-1 and poc/f2b stay byte-identical to their pins — any edit voids the staging-identity lemma and forces a k0 re-run). Stage-time attestation (PROPOSED-ASM-1413): the k1/k2 source TBox dirs are re-digested (kot-corpus-hash/1) against THIS record's registered pins, refusing post-freeze drift fail-closed; every payload carries an attestation block the pinned analysis validates — unattested or drifted payloads are rejected (INSTRUMENT-INVALID).
runner_constraints.kernel_arm: k0 = the kernel reference, INSTRUMENT gate only (already measured PASS pre-freeze, no unblinding: its outcome is the pinned rules-1 result)
runner_constraints.roles: frozen-design discipline (PROPOSED-ASM-1409): this DRAFT + inputs + mock were authored by the designer role 2026-07-12 and REWORKED 2026-07-12 to close the cross-vendor review; the definition->constraint mapping review is the INDEPENDENT reviewer role's (reviewer-1, authored neither definitions nor compiler, PROPOSED-ASM-1415); arms k1/k2 were NOT executed (runner refuses: ERR_NOT_REGISTERED pre-freeze, ERR_NOT_FROZEN on a DRAFT record; only k0, the parse-only smoke and synthetic mocks ran); FREEZE is the coordinator's (register PROPOSED-ASM-1400..1419, commit, re-verify shas, prereg-freeze.py); the registered $0 CPU run of k1+k2 (+k0 repeat) is the runner role's; verdict-gen mechanical grading; cross-vendor Codex audit; the analyst writes the readout with the envelope verbatim.
runner_constraints.stage_discipline: ORDER, enforced: (1) coordinator registers PROPOSED-ASM-1400..1419 (registry/assumptions.jsonl, append-only); (2) commit poc/rules-1-knull/* + analysis/rules_1_knull.py + docs/next/design/rules-1-knull-ablation.md, re-verify every sha in this record against the committed bytes (parse-smoke.json, def-constraint-mapping.json and mapping-review.json included); (3) prereg-freeze.py --experiment rules-1-knull-ablation --agent-id coordinator-1 (dry-run first); (4) runner role: python3 ablation_runner.py --arm k0 (gate repeat), then --arm k1 --registered and --arm k2 --registered (each payload attestation-stamped); (5) analysis/rules_1_knull.py --record <this record> over the three payloads + lint + smoke + mapping-review; (6) verdict-gen + audit; (7) phase-2b only after the rules-1 GPU readout carries /analysis/primary_pass true, on the pinned GPU harness with kernel cells reused (never regenerated).

title: [SUPERSEDED 2026-07-12 — DO NOT FREEZE: split into rules-1-knull-cert (CPU certificate leg, freeze-ready now) + rules-1-knull-hostlift (conditional GPU host-lift leg, blocked on rules-1 completing). Retained unmodified below as append-only history; ablation_runner.py REG_SELF was repointed to rules-1-knull-cert.json, so this record is on no execution path, and its pinned k0 report / runner shas describe pre-split bytes.] RULES-1-KNULL-ABLATION (MD-7 / c5, the kernel-specificity control) — does the RULES-1 non-inertness certificate (C_dec stated 1.0 / entailed 0.0, engine 858/858 vs third-party CLUTRR gold) SURVIVE byte-identical re-execution with the endorsed kinship TBox substituted by a KNULL plain-dictionary-sourced ruleset (=> kernel necessity UNSHOWN against this one compilation channel; the PROPOSED-ASM-1138 cap stays in force at this vertical, re-worded to name the measured channel) or COLLAPSE (=> KERNEL-ADVANTAGE-OVER-THIS-KNULL-COMPILATION at this vertical)? Sattolo-scrambled twin as the discrimination gate (must LOAD and fail, PROPOSED-ASM-1412); k0 shadow-staging identity gate measured PASS pre-freeze; per-payload digest attestation (PROPOSED-ASM-1413); parse-only smoke + independent mapping review pre-freeze (PROPOSED-ASM-1414/1415); phase-2b conditional host-lift leg pre-registered on the pinned rules-1 GPU harness. Deterministic $0 CPU primary; deflationary outcome gets equal prominence (the knull discipline verbatim).
